description: Adapter mapping for Mixtral MoE models. Extends the Llama adapter with MoE-specific components (router, experts, shared_expert). Mixtral uses the same base module structure as Llama for attention and normalization, but replaces the dense MLP with a block_sparse_moe module containing a gate (router) and multiple expert MLPs.

parameterized_indices.{i}: Layer index (0-based). Expands to all layers in the model (0..num_layers-1).
parameterized_indices.{j}: Expert index (0-based). Expands to all experts in the MoE layer (0..num_experts-1).

moe_module_mappings.note: The block_sparse_moe module replaces the dense MLP. Mapped to layers[i].mlp as the composite MLP-equivalent for this architecture.
Mixtral names expert projections w1/w2/w3. w1 = gate_proj.
w3 = up_proj (paired with gate in SwiGLU).

virtual_probes.capture_strategy: Captured from attention computation output. Requires attn_implementation='eager' or LSE reconstruction.
Captured as the input tensor to the transformer block (pre-hook on model.layers.{i}).
Captured as hidden states after attention add, before MoE layer.
Captured as the output tensor of the transformer block (post-hook on model.layers.{i}).
Captured from the router gate's linear output before softmax and top-k selection. Requires output_router_logits=True in model config.
Captured from the post-softmax, post-top-k routing decision. Contains selected expert indices and gating weights per token.
Not present in Mixtral (no shared expert). This virtual probe exists in the vocabulary for DeepSeek-V3-style architectures. For Mixtral, this component is absent and will not fire.

notes: Mixtral uses block_sparse_moe as the MoE layer, replacing the dense MLP entirely.
Expert weight naming in Mixtral: w1=gate_proj, w2=down_proj, w3=up_proj. This is NOT a typo — Mixtral's w2 is the down projection and w3 is the up projection.
The shared_expert canonical component is defined in the vocabulary but does not apply to Mixtral. It exists for forward compatibility with DeepSeek-V3 and similar shared+routed architectures.
Set output_router_logits=True in model config at attach time to expose routing logits through the model's output object.

The dense MLP mappings (mlp.gate_proj, mlp.up_proj, mlp.down_proj) from the Llama base adapter do NOT apply to Mixtral — those modules do not exist. The per-expert projections (experts[j].gate_proj, etc.) replace them.
